title: Crispy Japanese Steak Gyoza
description: Herbinfused, lean steak gyoza with a crunchy panfry finish, nostalgic Japanese flavor that's easy to make.

ingredients.name: lean minced beef
ingredients.notes: trim excess fat for leaner taste

instructions: Combine minced beef, shredded cabbage, chopped shiitake, sliced green onion, minced garlic, grated ginger, chopped cilantro, tamari, vinegar, sesame oil, salt, and pepper in a mixing bowl until well blended.
Place a small spoonful of the filling in the center of each gyoza wrapper.
Moisten the edges of the wrapper with water using a finger or a brush.
Fold the wrapper over the filling to form a half‑moon shape, pressing to seal and pinching the edges tightly.
Heat a non‑stick skillet over medium heat and add a thin layer of vegetable oil.
Arrange gyoza in a single layer, no overlap, and fry for 2‑3 minutes until the bottoms are golden brown.
Add about 2 tablespoons of water to the pan, cover tightly, and let the gyoza steam for 4 minutes until the water has evaporated.
Remove the lid, increase heat slightly, and fry for an additional 1–2 minutes until the bottoms are crisp again.
Transfer finished gyoza onto a paper towel to remove excess oil.
Serve hot with a dipping sauce of tamari, rice vinegar, and a drizzle of sesame oil, garnished with extra cilantro if desired.
